Definition: Technologies that shift the offense/defense balance toward protection rather than attack
Core Question: Does this make it easier to defend against harm than to cause harm?
Red Flags: Creates new attack surfaces; Enables cheaper harm than protection; Facilitates surveillance infrastructure
Example Pass: ZK proofs enabling verification without exposure; Social recovery wallets protecting against key loss
Example Fail: Drone swarms that could be weaponized; Surveillance AI that centralizes monitoring power

Definition: Technologies that can operate without a single controlling entity or trusted third party
Core Question: Can this operate without requiring trust that centralized actors will remain benevolent?
Red Flags: Requires trust in 'good guys' staying good; Single points of failure; Centralized upgrade authority
Example Pass: Bitcoin's permissionless consensus; IPFS content addressing
Example Fail: Trusted bridge multisigs; Centralized oracle providers

Definition: Technologies that enable broader participation in decisions and resist capture by small groups
Core Question: Does this enable more people to participate meaningfully in governance and coordination?
Red Flags: Concentrates capability in small groups; Creates plutocratic dynamics; Excludes participation based on resources
Example Pass: Quadratic voting reducing whale dominance; Community Notes finding cross-partisan consensus
Example Fail: Token-weighted voting with whale concentration; Closed governance councils

Definition: Technologies that accelerate defense capabilities faster than offense capabilities
Core Question: Does this make defense grow faster than offense over time?
Red Flags: Equally useful for harmful applications; Offense benefits more from improvements; Creates arms race dynamics
Example Pass: Far-UVC (kills pathogens universally without targeting); Air filtering (pure defense)
